steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Central Ideas and Details question. The task is to identify the main idea of the whole passage. The passage describes shimmering behavior in giant honeybees, notes it occurs when hornets hover nearby, and ends with the researchers’ hypothesis about what that behavior represents. The main idea must capture both the behavior and the researchers’ interpretation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Researchers are unsure how giant honeybees defend against predators larger than hornets.: The passage says shimmering is not observed with larger predators, but it never suggests researchers are uncertain about what defenses are used against them. This misreads the passage’s point. ✗
  • Choice B — Researchers think that shimmering in giant honeybees is a specific defense against hornets.: The passage states researchers “hypothesize that this behavior is a specialized defense response to hornets.” This choice captures that hypothesis precisely and is the central claim the passage is building toward. ✓
  • Choice C — Hornets are known to be the main predator of giant honeybees.: The passage mentions hornets as one predator that triggers shimmering, but never claims they are the main predator. This also ignores shimmering entirely, which is the text’s focus. ✗
  • Choice D — Several different species of insects use shimmering to defend against hornets.: The passage only discusses giant honeybees — it makes no claims about other insect species at all. ✗
That matches Choice B — Researchers think that shimmering in giant honeybees is a specific defense against hornets..
